what_it_does: Implements the Settings workspace UI for the Counterpart Studio: a tabbed settings page with sections for provider API keys, live model list fetching, image model selection, voice/audio model routing, studio reasoning defaults, Ollama local model discovery, and an about panel. Keys are persisted to localStorage; model data is read from window.MS_DATA and refreshed via window.MS_FETCH.
observations: React hooks are aliased with an S suffix (useStateS, useEffectS, useMemoS, useCallbackS) destructured from React.
The file depends on globals: window.MS_DATA, window.MS_FETCH, window.ViContext, plus assumed components Icon, Btn, Chip, Field, Panel, ProviderBadge, ViMark.
API keys are stored in localStorage under the prefix ms_key_.
ProviderCard fetches live model lists on first expand and falls back to registry models when live fetch fails or is unavailable.
useProviderModels merges live API results with curated registry entries, annotating each model with provider, live flag, and tier.
Sections filter the merged model list by kind: image/image-edit, voice/tts/transcribe, text/vision.
Models are classified and sorted by tier: flagship, current, preview, mini, legacy.
OllamaSection supports a configurable base URL and a re-discover action.
Several buttons are non-functional placeholders (Encrypt, Export, Clear all, Re-discover models, Use for kind, info icon).
The AboutSection describes Counterpart as a sequence-first design and build studio.
Toggle manages only local visual state and does not persist or propagate changes.
standouts: The custom useProviderModels hook that unifies live provider model lists with curated registry metadata across all providers.
Tier-based model classification used consistently for sorting, filtering, and visual chips.
Local-first routing concept surfaced in the Ollama section.
Sequence-first / anti-pattern memory language in the About panel.
conventions: Global singleton data/fetch services attached to window.
Inline style objects for dynamic theming combined with utility className tokens.
Component composition via shared primitives: Panel, Field, Btn, Chip, Icon, ProviderBadge.
concerns: API keys are stored in plain localStorage, which is not a secure secret store.
Several buttons have placeholder onClick handlers with no implemented behavior.
Toggle state is ephemeral and not wired to any persisted setting.
Provider metadata (name, site, hint, docs) is hardcoded inside ProviderCard.
onSave invalidates the live list but does not trigger an automatic re-fetch.
No input validation beyond checking key length > 8.
useProviderModels fetches every provider on mount regardless of which section is active.
Inline styles mixed with utility classes may reduce maintainability.
notes: A settings workspace that wires global provider/model registries into a multi-section configuration UI, with live model discovery and localStorage key persistence.
questions: Where are window.MS_DATA, window.MS_FETCH, and the shared UI primitives defined?
Is there a production backend proxy to handle Anthropic browser CORS?
Are the placeholder buttons intended to be implemented later?
How are default model selections persisted beyond component state?
What is the intended security model for localStorage API keys versus encrypted storage?
Why are React hooks aliased with an S suffix?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the header comment says it handles API keys, model routing, voice config, and Ollama discovery, and the code implements all of those sections.
apparent_themes: Provider-agnostic model marketplace inside a single studio.
Live data preferred over static configuration, with registry fallback.
intention_signals: The file is trying to give users a unified control surface for all LLM providers and modalities, letting them paste keys, refresh live model catalogs, and set defaults per modality, while keeping the implementation browser-local and mock-friendly.
novelty: The tier-based model classification and cross-provider, kind-filtered model pickers (image, voice, reasoning) that merge live API results with curated registry metadata.
contribution: A self-contained settings workspace pattern that demonstrates how to surface provider keys, live model discovery, modality-specific defaults, and local-model integration in one navigable UI, plus a reusable tier-sorting/model-row abstraction.
summary_for_embedding: React settings workspace component for Counterpart Studio that manages browser-local API keys, refreshes live provider model lists, and lets users configure image, voice, reasoning, and Ollama models through kind-filtered, tier-sorted pickers.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a single, provider-agnostic settings UI where users can authenticate providers, discover available models, and set per-modality defaults.

symbols.does: Reads a provider's API key from localStorage using the ms_key_ prefix.
symbols.description: Utility that returns the stored key string for a given provider id, or an empty string if none exists.

symbols.does: Writes a provider's API key to localStorage under the ms_key_ prefix.
symbols.description: Utility that persists the raw key value for a provider id with no encryption or validation.

symbols.does: Renders the provider-keys section with bulk live model refresh and per-provider cards.
symbols.description: Top-level settings section that lets users refresh model lists across all configured providers and review live vs fallback status before expanding individual provider cards.

symbols.does: Sequentially fetches live model lists for every provider and aggregates the results.
symbols.description: Async loop over window.MS_DATA.providers calling window.MS_FETCH.fetchModels, updating a running results map so the UI shows per-provider progress.

symbols.does: Collapsible card for a single provider showing key input, connection test, and available models.
symbols.description: Displays provider metadata, an editable API key field (except Ollama), a test-connection button, and a grid of models sourced live or from the registry.

symbols.does: Fetches the live model list for the ProviderCard's provider.
symbols.description: Calls window.MS_FETCH.fetchModels and stores the result, setting a fetching flag for UI feedback.

symbols.does: Tests the provider connection by fetching its model list.
symbols.description: Sets a pending state, calls fetchModels, and marks the test as ok or err based on the response; also updates the displayed live list.

symbols.does: Persists a key value and resets related UI state.
symbols.description: Updates local component state and localStorage for the provider key, clears the test status, and invalidates the cached live model list.

symbols.does: Custom hook that loads and merges live and registry models across all providers.
symbols.description: Fetches each provider's models on mount, then combines live API results with curated registry entries, annotating each model with provider, live flag, and tier for use by kind-filtered sections.

symbols.does: Returns a Chip component for a given model tier.
symbols.description: Maps tier names (flagship, current, mini, preview, legacy) to a Chip kind and label.

symbols.does: Sorts models by tier priority then alphabetically.
symbols.description: Orders models using a fixed tier ranking (flagship → current → preview → mini → legacy) and falls back to name/id comparison.

symbols.does: Compact selectable row rendering a model with provider, tier, and live status.
symbols.description: Reusable one-line model button used by audio pickers, showing name/id, tier chip, live/registry chip, and optional selection outline.

symbols.does: Renders image and image-edit model configuration.
symbols.description: Filters merged models to image kinds, sorts by tier, toggles legacy visibility, and displays image model cards plus per-job default controls.

symbols.does: Collapsible card for an image or image-edit model.
symbols.description: Shows provider, name, id, cost/latency chips, and expandable strengths/limits/inputs details.

symbols.does: Renders voice/audio model selection and voice behavior settings.
symbols.description: Lets users pick a provider, then lists bidi voice, STT, and TTS models; includes voice, language, turn detection, and wake phrase fields.

symbols.does: Renders a titled grid of audio models with selection support.
symbols.description: Sub-component used by VoiceSection to display bidi, transcribe, or TTS models using ModelRow.

symbols.does: Renders text/vision reasoning model defaults and Vi behavior toggles.
symbols.description: Filters merged models to text/vision kinds, groups by tier, lets user set a default reasoning model, and shows a list of Vi behavior options.

symbols.does: Renders a tier-labeled list of reasoning models with default selection.
symbols.description: Displays models of a single tier as selectable rows, highlighting the default and recommended model.

symbols.does: Renders local Ollama discovery and local-first routing options.
symbols.description: Provides a base URL input, re-discover button, connection/model count chips, a grid of local models, and toggles for local-first routing.

symbols.does: Fetches the current Ollama model list from a configurable base URL.
symbols.description: Async function that calls window.MS_FETCH.fetchModels for ollama with the baseUrl option and updates live connection state.

symbols.does: Renders the about panel for Vi / Counterpart.
symbols.description: Displays branding, version, and a short manifesto describing Counterpart as a sequence-first design and build studio.

symbols.does: Renders a simple on/off switch button with local state.
symbols.description: Visual toggle component used for behavior options; manages its own state and does not persist or propagate changes.
